post.content: I recently took an assessment and got this as the question. Anyone know how we can solve this ? I used hashmap to group the indexes with the same mean, but it didn\'t solve all test cases. The order of the result array was messed up. \n![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/19ac89e8-8acb-4d65-8728-cca13d93db92_1605424898.596867.png)\n

```python [printA-python3]
from collections import defaultdict
def Solution(A):
    map=defaultdict(list)
    for i,lst in enumerate(A):
        sum = 0
        for l in lst:
            sum += l

        mean = sum/len(lst)
        map[mean].append(i)


    ans = [l for l in map.values()]
    ans = sorted(ans,key = lambda x: min(x) )
    return ans
```

I think if you iterate over the groups in order and store it in a LinkedHashMap, your  map entries/groups will be sorted in the order of array groups.i.e. Group containing smaller array indices would come first.
